ip_outcome_note: Cinderella reference confirmed as intentional Wizards of the Coast top-down fairy-tale design, not external-IP infringement risk. Throne of Eldraine is Wizards' explicit Grimms' Fairy Tales + Arthurian set [CBR interview with Mark Rosewater]. Wizards designer Mark Rosewater's 'More Odds & Ends: Throne of Eldraine' (2019-10-21) lists 'the pumpkin carriage' among the iconic fairy-tale objects the set's artifacts draw from, and states verbatim that the Mouse creature type 'exists only because the Enchanted Carriage needed white Mice for flavor' — confirming the card is the pumpkin-coach-plus-mice Cinderella reference by design. Cinderella itself is public domain (Charles Perrault 1697 / Brothers Grimm 1812); no external IP risk. ip_verified set to true; no ## IP callout block required (Wizards-internal top-down design, not a Universes Beyond crossover).

trivia_bullets.text: Throne of Eldraine (released 2019-10-04) is Wizards' top-down designed homage set drawing on Grimms' Fairy Tales and Arthurian legend; Mark Rosewater said he had been 'waiting 10 years to do a Grimm's world' [CBR: 'Grimms' Fairy Tales Meet Arthurian Legend in New Magic: The Gathering Set'].

Wizards designer Mark Rosewater explicitly groups 'the pumpkin carriage' with the other fairy-tale objects that drove the set's artifact design — 'those had a lot of objects in them (Excalibur, the Holy Grail, the Round Table, the glass slipper, the pumpkin carriage, the glass casket, Pinocchio, the Gingerbread Man, the Magic Mirror, etc.)' [Wizards: 'More Odds & Ends: Throne of Eldraine' 2019-10-21].
sources: magic.wizards.com: More Odds & Ends: Throne of Eldraine (2019-10-21)

trivia_bullets.text: The Mouse creature type was invented for this card specifically — Rosewater writes verbatim: 'This creature type exists only because the Enchanted Carriage needed white Mice for flavor.' The two 1/1 white Mouse tokens directly reference the mice-turned-coachmen of the Cinderella tale [Wizards: 'More Odds & Ends: Throne of Eldraine' 2019-10-21].
sources: magic.wizards.com: More Odds & Ends: Throne of Eldraine (2019-10-21)

trivia_bullets.text: Rosewater himself uses the card as a modular-flavor example in his earlier design diary, gesturing at how fairy-tale tropes were built to remix: 'my Gingerbrute and my Flaxen Intruder crew my Enchanted Carriage' [Wizards: 'Odds & Ends: Throne of Eldraine' 2019-10-14]. Artist: Zoltan Boros [scryfall.com card page metadata via search results].
sources: magic.wizards.com: Odds & Ends: Throne of Eldraine (2019-10-14)
Scryfall card page (metadata via search engine summary)

trivia_bullets.text: 5-mana 4/4 Artifact - Vehicle, Crew 2, enters with two 1/1 white Mouse tokens [search-result summary of Scryfall card data]. EDHREC: included in roughly 2,640 of 8.78M decks (~0.03% overall inclusion); the strongest commander synergy is Mabel, Heir to Cragflame at ~10% inclusion (Mouse tribal, Bloomburrow), followed by Greasefang, Okiba Boss (vehicles) at ~5.1% [EDHREC: enchanted-carriage].
sources: Card metadata via search-result summaries (Scryfall, AetherHub, Card Kingdom, Gatherer)

trivia_bullets.text: Community deckbuilding reception treats the card as the foundation of post-Bloomburrow Mouse tribal: TappedOut, Archidekt and Moxfield Mabel, Heir to Cragflame Mouse-tribal lists routinely run it as 'the first mouse card ever made' [community deck-page descriptions]. Direct Reddit-thread results were sparse for this query — sentiment here is sourced from deckbuilding sites rather than r/EDH discourse threads.
sources: tappedout.net: Mabel Mouse Tribal and Equipment
moxfield.com: Mouse Tribal (I gave it a knife)

related_cards.name: Turn into a Pumpkin
related_cards.note: Same set, same Cinderella-pumpkin flavor cycle — paired Cinderella reference on the spell side (transformation back into a pumpkin at midnight).

related_cards.name: Fairy Guidemother
related_cards.note: Same set, identified in folklore-mapping coverage as the fairy godmother who enables the Cinderella story this card depicts the conjuring-result of.

related_cards.name: Midnight Clock
related_cards.note: Same set, the midnight-tolls beat of the Cinderella story — coheres with the twilight/about-to-break-magic mood the Vision pass flagged on this card.

related_cards.name: Mabel, Heir to Cragflame
related_cards.note: Top EDHREC commander synergy partner (~10% inclusion); the canonical Mouse-tribal commander that brought Enchanted Carriage back into format-relevant decks.

related_cards.name: Greasefang, Okiba Boss
related_cards.note: Second-highest EDHREC synergy (~5.1% inclusion); reanimator-vehicles commander that wants any cheap-ish artifact vehicle.
